symbols: enthroned ruler figure — embodiment of the commune and just governance
winged figures above — theological or cardinal virtues guiding government
procession of citizens — the well-ordered civic body united under good rule
scales and cords — justice binding the community together in equity
description: A large fresco depicting an enthroned male figure in dark robes at center-right, flanked by seated female allegorical figures representing virtues. Above, winged figures hover. At left, another enthroned figure holds scales. A procession of citizens in varied dress stretches across the lower register, linked by a cord. The composition is arranged in a horizontal band across an architectural wall, with a Latin inscription running along the base. The palette is warm earth tones with gold and blue accents.
